source: doloop canonical research (7 streams + completeness critic)

entries.name: Long Method
entries.description: A method or function has grown so long that it does too many things and is hard to understand, reuse, test, and change.
entries.detection: Executable-LOC per function body > 30-50 (warn) / > 60-100 (fail) via AST function-node span; or cyclomatic complexity > 10; or statement count > 25-50.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Function on cohesive blocks; Replace Temp with Query.

entries.name: Large Class / God Class
entries.description: A class is trying to do too much, accumulating too many fields, methods, and responsibilities.
entries.detection: Methods/class > 20, fields > 15, or class LOC > 500 (warn) / > 1000 (fail). Lanza God Class: WMC>=47 AND TCC<0.33 AND ATFD>5.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Class / Extract Subclass / Extract Interface.

entries.name: Duplicated Code
entries.description: The same or nearly-identical code structure appears in more than one place.
entries.detection: Token/AST clone detection (Type-1/2/3): >=6 duplicated statements or >=40-50 tokens with >85% similarity appearing >=2 times.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Function; Pull Up Method; Form Template Method.

entries.name: Long Parameter List
entries.description: A method takes so many parameters that callers struggle to understand and supply them correctly.
entries.detection: Parameter count > 4 (warn) / > 6 (fail). PMD 10, Checkstyle 7, pylint 5, ESLint 3.
entries.fix_hint: Introduce Parameter Object; Preserve Whole Object; Remove Flag Argument.

entries.name: Global Data
entries.description: Data accessible from anywhere can be mutated by any code, making changes impossible to trace.
entries.detection: Count module/global-scope mutable bindings, public static mutable fields, singletons with mutable state.
entries.fix_hint: Encapsulate Variable; reduce scope; pass state explicitly.

entries.name: Mutable Data
entries.description: Data updated in place can be changed where another part assumed it would not.
entries.detection: Reassigned vars that could be const/final, shared mutable objects mutated by reference, public mutable fields. Reader judges essential vs incidental.
entries.fix_hint: Encapsulate Variable; Split Variable; Change Reference to Value.

entries.name: Divergent Change
entries.description: One class is changed in many different ways for many different reasons.
entries.detection: A single file/class touched by commits addressing >=3 distinct concern clusters; low cohesion (LCOM4>1). Reader confirms unrelated reasons.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Class to split divergent contexts; Split Phase.

entries.name: Shotgun Surgery
entries.description: A single conceptual change forces many small edits scattered across many classes.
entries.detection: Version-history: a logical change co-modifies N>5 files; high fan-out dispersion. Reader confirms one change.
entries.fix_hint: Move Function / Move Field to gather behavior; Combine Functions into Class.

entries.name: Feature Envy
entries.description: A method is more interested in another class's data than its own.
entries.detection: Per method, foreign accesses to one other class vs host; flag foreign>host. Lanza: ATFD>3 AND LAA<0.33 AND FDP<=3.
entries.fix_hint: Move Function to envied class; Extract then Move.

entries.name: Data Clumps
entries.description: The same group of data items keeps appearing together in fields and parameter lists.
entries.detection: Identical ordered groups of >=3 params/fields recurring across >=2 signatures (name+type tuple). Warn at 2, fail at 3+.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Class; Introduce Parameter Object.

entries.name: Primitive Obsession
entries.description: Primitives represent domain concepts that deserve their own small types.
entries.detection: Type-code constants, primitive fields named like domain quantities without wrappers, arrays as records. Reader confirms a concept is a bare primitive.
entries.fix_hint: Replace Primitive with Object; Replace Type Code with Subclasses.

entries.name: Repeated Switches / Switch Statement
entries.description: The same conditional switch on a type code recurs in multiple places.
entries.detection: >=2 switch/if-else ladders on the same discriminant. Warn at 2, fail at 3+. Reader confirms polymorphism is right.
entries.fix_hint: Replace Conditional with Polymorphism; State/Strategy; dispatch table.

entries.name: Loops
entries.description: An explicit imperative loop is used where a collection pipeline would express intent more clearly.
entries.detection: Loop bodies matching map/filter/reduce shapes. Reader confirms pure transformation, no early-exit complications.
entries.fix_hint: Replace Loop with Pipeline; Extract Function.

entries.name: Parallel Inheritance Hierarchies
entries.description: Adding a subclass to one hierarchy forces a matching subclass in another.
entries.detection: Two hierarchies whose subclass name prefixes correspond 1:1 and co-add historically.
entries.fix_hint: Move Function/Field so one hierarchy refers to the other; collapse.

entries.name: Lazy Class / Lazy Element
entries.description: A class or element no longer pulls its weight.
entries.detection: Class with <=1-2 methods and few fields and few references, or a forwarding-only function.
entries.fix_hint: Inline Class; Collapse Hierarchy; Inline Function.

entries.name: Speculative Generality
entries.description: Machinery added for hypothetical future needs that nobody uses today.
entries.detection: Abstract types with a single implementor, unused params/hooks, methods with only test callers, single-impl factory/strategy. Reader confirms no current use.
entries.fix_hint: Collapse Hierarchy; Inline; drop unused params; Remove Dead Code.

entries.name: Temporary Field
entries.description: An instance field is meaningful only in certain circumstances.
entries.detection: Field assigned/read only inside one method cluster (used by <50% of methods). Reader confirms scratch state vs lazy cache.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Class; Introduce Special Case (Null Object).

entries.name: Message Chains
entries.description: A client navigates a long chain of object-to-object calls.
entries.detection: AST chain of >=3 consecutive accessor calls. Warn at depth 3, fail at 4+. Exclude fluent-builder allowlist.
entries.fix_hint: Hide Delegate; Extract then Move Function.

entries.name: Middle Man
entries.description: A class delegates almost all of its work to another.
entries.detection: Pure-delegating methods / total methods > 50% (warn) / >75% (fail).
entries.fix_hint: Remove Middle Man; Inline Function.

entries.name: Insider Trading / Inappropriate Intimacy
entries.description: Two modules trade private data and reach into each other's internals.
entries.detection: Bidirectional coupling: A accesses B's privates AND B accesses A's. Warn when mutual foreign accesses > 3.
entries.fix_hint: Move Function/Field; Hide Delegate; Replace Inheritance with Delegation.

entries.name: Alternative Classes with Different Interfaces
entries.description: Two classes do the same job but expose different method names/signatures.
entries.detection: High behavior overlap but mismatched names (behavior Jaccard high, name match low), or >=80% identical bodies. Reader confirms equivalence.
entries.fix_hint: Change Function Declaration to align; Extract Superclass.

entries.name: Data Class
entries.description: A class is just fields with getters/setters and no behavior.
entries.detection: Methods exclusively accessors while other classes manipulate its fields. Lanza: low WOC, high NOAP+NOAM. Exclude DTO/record allowlist.
entries.fix_hint: Move Function in; Encapsulate Collection; Remove Setting Method.

entries.name: Refused Bequest / Rebellious Hierarchy
entries.description: A subclass uses only some of what it inherits and overrides-to-empty/throw the rest.
entries.detection: Override to throw Unsupported/NotImplemented/no-op, or uses <50% of inherited members. Reader decides push-down vs replace-inheritance.
entries.fix_hint: Push Down Method/Field; Replace Subclass/Superclass with Delegate.

entries.name: Comments (Deodorant)
entries.description: Comments used to explain confusing code that should explain itself.
entries.detection: High inline-comment density inside method bodies, or a comment preceding an extractable block. Reader distinguishes deodorant from why/contract docs.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Function; Rename; Introduce Assertion.

entries.name: Incomplete Library Class
entries.description: A reused library class lacks a method you need but cannot modify.
entries.detection: Many local util functions whose first argument is the same third-party type. Reader confirms missing library capability.
entries.fix_hint: Introduce Foreign Method; Introduce Local Extension.

entries.name: Mysterious Name
entries.description: A name fails to clearly and honestly say what it is or does.
entries.detection: Single-letter/numbered identifiers outside tiny scopes, abbreviations not in allowlist, names <3 chars for non-loop vars. Reader judges misdescribing names.
entries.fix_hint: Rename Variable/Field; Change Function Declaration.

entries.name: Dead Code / Unused Artifacts
entries.description: Code never executed or never referenced.
entries.detection: Reachability + use-def: unreferenced privates, unused locals/params, code after return/throw, constant-false conditions. ruff F401/F841; ESLint no-unreachable.
entries.fix_hint: Remove Dead Code; enable the linter rule in CI.

entries.name: High Cyclomatic Complexity
entries.description: Too many independent linear paths through control flow.
entries.detection: Decision points +1. Flag CC>10 (warn) / >15 (fail). PMD 10; ESLint 20.
entries.fix_hint: Extract branches into named helpers; guard clauses; polymorphism.

entries.name: High Cognitive Complexity
entries.description: Control flow hard to follow due to nesting and breaks in linear reading.
entries.detection: +1 per linear-flow break plus nesting penalty. Flag score >15.
entries.fix_hint: Flatten nesting with early returns; extract nested blocks.

entries.name: High NPath Complexity
entries.description: Excessive number of distinct acyclic execution paths.
entries.detection: Multiply path counts recursively. Flag NPath>200.
entries.fix_hint: Extract sub-methods; table/strategy dispatch.

entries.name: Deep Nesting
entries.description: Control structures nested too deeply, producing arrow-shaped code.
entries.detection: Max block nesting depth >3 (PMD); ESLint max-depth 4. Flag conditions with >4 boolean operators.
entries.fix_hint: Guard clauses; extract inner blocks; lookup tables.

entries.name: Lack of Cohesion (High LCOM)
entries.description: Methods operate on disjoint sets of fields, bundling unrelated responsibilities.
entries.detection: LCOM(CK)>0; LCOM4 connected components >1.
entries.fix_hint: Split the class along connected method/field clusters.

entries.name: Excessive Coupling (High CBO)
entries.description: A class depends on or is depended on by too many other classes.
entries.detection: CBO = distinct non-inheritance classes referenced. Flag >13-20; PMD 20.
entries.fix_hint: Introduce interfaces / dependency inversion; Facade.

entries.name: High Fan-out / Fan-in / Hub
entries.description: A module calls or is called by too many others, concentrating change risk.
entries.detection: Fan-out>~7, or both CA and CE in top decile (hub).
entries.fix_hint: Introduce mediating facades; split high-fan-out modules.

entries.name: Too Many Methods / Fields
entries.description: A class declares an excessive number of methods or fields.
entries.detection: PMD TooManyMethods 10; TooManyFields 15; WMC>=47.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Class along cohesive subsets.

entries.name: Too Many Branches
entries.description: A function contains too many decision branches.
entries.detection: pylint max-branches default 12.
entries.fix_hint: Extract branch groups; dispatch tables.

entries.name: Too Many Local Variables
entries.description: A function declares too many local variables.
entries.detection: pylint max-locals default 15.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Method; group related locals into an object.

entries.name: Too Many Nested Callbacks
entries.description: Asynchronous callbacks nested too deeply.
entries.detection: ESLint max-nested-callbacks default 10 (commonly 3).
entries.fix_hint: Flatten with named functions, Promises, async/await.

entries.name: High Halstead Volume / Difficulty / Effort
entries.description: Operator/operand counts indicate large mental volume.
entries.detection: V=(N1+N2)*log2(n1+n2); D=(n1/2)*(N2/n2); E=D*V. Flag outliers.
entries.fix_hint: Decompose; reduce distinct operators/operands.

entries.name: Low Maintainability Index
entries.description: Composite of Halstead volume, CC, and LOC indicates costly maintenance.
entries.fix_hint: Attack the dominant driver via extraction and simplification.

entries.name: Long Class File / Excessive Lines
entries.description: A source file or class exceeds a sane line budget.
entries.detection: ESLint max-lines 300; PMD ExcessiveClassLength 1000; Checkstyle FileLength 2000.
entries.fix_hint: Split by responsibility; extract collaborators.

entries.name: Spaghetti Code
entries.description: Tangled control flow, global state, little modularity.
entries.detection: DECOR card: high LOC, no params, global use, deep nesting, no polymorphism; CC>15-25, LOC>100, depth>5.
entries.fix_hint: Restructure into cohesive methods; classes/polymorphism.

entries.name: Functional Decomposition (OO antipattern)
entries.description: Procedural design forced into verb-named classes with one dominant method.
entries.detection: DECOR card: verb class name, single dominant method, globals-as-fields, near-zero polymorphism.
entries.fix_hint: Redesign around cohesive objects with state+behavior.

entries.name: Excessive Public Count / Broad API Surface
entries.description: A class exposes too many public members.
entries.detection: PMD ExcessivePublicCount 45; high RFC.
entries.fix_hint: Narrow the public interface; split into role interfaces.

entries.name: Cyclic Dependencies
entries.description: Modules depend on each other directly or transitively (ADP violation).
entries.detection: Import graph cycle detection (Tarjan SCC); flag SCC size >1.
entries.fix_hint: Dependency Inversion; extract shared part to a third module.

entries.name: Incomplete Abstraction
entries.description: An abstraction lacks complementary methods (push but no pop).
entries.detection: Check complementary pairs (add/remove, open/close, start/stop) where only one is declared.
entries.fix_hint: Add the missing complementary method(s).

entries.name: Unnecessary Abstraction
entries.description: An abstraction introduced that is not actually needed.
entries.detection: Constants-only class, or a type referenced 0-1 times (no non-test callers).
entries.fix_hint: Inline or remove; collapse constants into using class/enum.

entries.name: Deficient Encapsulation
entries.description: Member accessibility is more permissive than required.
entries.detection: Public non-final fields; public methods never called externally.
entries.fix_hint: Tighten access modifiers to the minimum required.

entries.name: Leaky Encapsulation
entries.description: An abstraction exposes implementation details through its interface.
entries.detection: Public getter returns a mutable internal collection without copy; internal types in signatures.
entries.fix_hint: Return copies/immutable views; intent-revealing operations.

entries.name: Unexploited Encapsulation
entries.description: Client uses explicit type checks instead of existing polymorphism.
entries.detection: instanceof/typeof/GetType chains (>=2) over subtypes of a polymorphic hierarchy.
entries.fix_hint: Replace explicit type checks with a polymorphic method.

entries.name: Unnecessary Hierarchy
entries.description: Inheritance applied where composition would suffice.
entries.detection: Subclasses that add/override nothing, or hierarchy used only for data variation.
entries.fix_hint: Collapse the hierarchy; composition or parameterization.

entries.name: Unfactored Hierarchy
entries.description: Sibling classes share copy-pasted code that should be pulled up.
entries.detection: Clone detection scoped to siblings: near-identical bodies (>=85%) in >=2 siblings.
entries.fix_hint: Pull duplicated members up to the superclass.

entries.name: Wide Hierarchy
entries.description: An inheritance hierarchy is too wide.
entries.detection: NOC above threshold (e.g. >10) where subgroups share features.
entries.fix_hint: Introduce intermediate abstractions.

entries.name: Deep Hierarchy
entries.description: An inheritance hierarchy is excessively deep.
entries.detection: DIT above threshold (e.g. >5 for non-framework code).
entries.fix_hint: Flatten; favor composition over deep inheritance.
sources: Suryanarayana et al. (2014); Chidamber & Kemerer DIT

entries.name: Broken Hierarchy
entries.description: Supertype and subtype do not share an is-a relationship.
entries.detection: Subtype shares no client-facing behavior and is never used polymorphically. Requires reader judgment on is-a.
entries.fix_hint: Replace inheritance with composition/delegation.

entries.name: Multipath Hierarchy
entries.description: A subtype inherits both directly and indirectly from a supertype (diamond).
entries.detection: Supertype reachable from a subtype via >=2 distinct paths in the inheritance DAG.
entries.fix_hint: Single inheritance path; interfaces or composition.
entries.languages: C++, Python, Eiffel; multiple default methods

entries.name: Cyclic Hierarchy
entries.description: A supertype depends on one of its subtypes.
entries.detection: Reference from a base type to any descendant type (base->derived edge).
entries.fix_hint: Invert the dependency; introduce an abstraction/callback.

entries.name: Single Responsibility Principle Violation
entries.description: A class has more than one reason to change.
entries.detection: Low cohesion (LCOM4>1) plus high WMC; or Divergent Change history.
entries.fix_hint: Extract Class so each has one reason to change.

entries.name: Open/Closed Principle Violation
entries.description: Adding a variant requires modifying existing code rather than extending.
entries.detection: Same discriminator switch repeated across methods/files; Shotgun Surgery churn.
entries.fix_hint: Introduce polymorphism/Strategy.

entries.name: Liskov Substitution Principle Violation
entries.description: A subtype cannot substitute for its base type without altering correctness.
entries.detection: Override throwing Unsupported/NotImplemented, narrowing args, covariant null. Full contract checks need a reader.
entries.fix_hint: Honor the base contract or use composition.

entries.name: Interface Segregation Principle Violation
entries.description: Clients depend on interface methods they do not use.
entries.detection: Interface NOM>~10 where implementers stub >30% or clients call a small fraction.
entries.fix_hint: Split into role-specific interfaces.

entries.name: Dependency Inversion Principle Violation
entries.description: High-level policy depends directly on low-level concretes.
entries.detection: Policy class news/imports concrete low-level classes (DB drivers, IO) rather than interfaces.
entries.fix_hint: Depend on abstractions; inject concretes.

entries.name: Anemic Domain Model
entries.description: Domain objects are data containers while logic lives in service classes.
entries.detection: Domain package mostly Data Classes plus a thick service layer with Feature Envy toward them.
entries.fix_hint: Relocate business rules into entities; thin the service layer.

entries.name: Golden Hammer
entries.description: A familiar tool/pattern is applied to every problem regardless of fit.
entries.detection: Reader: is this tool/pattern a poor fit, used mainly out of familiarity?
entries.fix_hint: Choose tools by problem fit; diversify.

entries.name: Poltergeist
entries.description: Short-lived stateless classes that only invoke methods on others.
entries.detection: Class with NOF<=1, transient lifecycle, high pass-through ratio.
entries.fix_hint: Inline Class into the classes it orchestrates.

entries.name: Boat Anchor
entries.description: Software/dependency retained though it serves no purpose.
entries.detection: Declared dependencies/imports with zero references; vendored libs not linked.
entries.fix_hint: Remove unused dependencies and components.

entries.name: Dead End
entries.description: A reused component is forked and can no longer be maintained upstream.
entries.detection: Locally patched third-party/vendored code (diff vs upstream non-empty).
entries.fix_hint: Contribute upstream or wrap/extend rather than fork.

entries.name: Magic Number / Magic Literal
entries.description: Unexplained numeric/string literals and hardcoded paths/URLs/timeouts embedded inline.
entries.detection: Numeric literals outside {-1,0,1,2} in conditionals/arithmetic without a named binding; absolute paths and full URLs in code.
entries.fix_hint: Promote literals to named constants or configuration.

entries.name: Empty Catch / Silent Error Swallowing
entries.description: An exception is caught and silently discarded.
entries.detection: Catch/except body empty, pass, continue, bare return None; broad except. PMD EmptyCatchBlock; SonarQube S108/S2486.
entries.fix_hint: Catch the narrowest type; log or re-raise with context.

entries.name: Commented-out Code
entries.description: Source disabled by commenting rather than deletion.
entries.detection: Strip the comment marker and parse; if it parses as code (or matches code-shaped heuristics) flag. SonarQube S125.
entries.fix_hint: Delete it; version control preserves history.

entries.name: Missing Abstraction (encoded-string data)
entries.description: Encoded strings/primitives used instead of a class for the concept.
entries.detection: String-encoded structured data parsed into fields; repeated primitive groups (>=3 across >=2 methods).
entries.fix_hint: Introduce a value object (Replace Data Value with Object).

entries.name: The || Operator Plague
entries.description: Missing/malformed data masked behind long chains of ||/?? fallbacks.
entries.detection: >=2 chained ||/?? terminating in a literal default. 2+ = warn; 3+ = fail.
entries.fix_hint: Validate once at the boundary; raise on absence; at most one default.

entries.name: Variable variable_names
entries.description: Excessive dynamic/computed property access defeating static analysis.
entries.detection: Dynamic member-access ratio >15%, or any eval/exec/Function to build identifiers.
entries.fix_hint: Explicit fields or a typed map/enum.

entries.name: Content in Code
entries.description: Long-form documentation embedded in source, drowning logic.
entries.detection: Comment lines >40% AND a single comment block >30 lines, plus prose detection.
entries.fix_hint: Move narrative to docs/; leave terse why-comments.

entries.name: Field Mapping Madness
entries.description: Converter layers translate between divergent field names instead of fixing the schema.
entries.detection: Functions predominantly out.X=in.Y rename assignments; >=3 mappers or same field under >=3 names.
entries.fix_hint: Normalize the schema at the source; delete shims.

entries.name: Bandaid Fixes
entries.description: Converters/guards/middleware stacked around a symptom instead of root cause.
entries.detection: Wrapper whose only logic is null-guarding/reshaping an unchanged callee; same file in >=3 successive fix commits.
entries.fix_hint: Trace to the originating contract and fix there.

entries.name: File Version Proliferation
entries.description: foo-v2/foo-FINAL variants created instead of using git branches.
entries.detection: Basename regex for v\d+/copy/final/clean/backup/old suffixes; two files with identical stem differing only by such suffix.
entries.fix_hint: Delete the variant; use git branches/tags.

entries.name: Fake Data Generation / Mock Data Left In
entries.description: Invented placeholder content or mocks/stubs left in production paths.
entries.detection: Sentinel literals (lorem ipsum, John Doe, example.com), mock/stub/fixture identifiers, canned returns, test URLs/keys; especially in except branches.
entries.fix_hint: On failure propagate the error; confine mocks to test dirs.

entries.name: Template Defaulting
entries.description: Generic template text returned in place of real content.
entries.detection: Compare output against template registry; flag unfilled tokens ({{ }}, <placeholder>, TODO: fill).
entries.fix_hint: Fail explicitly when real content is unavailable.

entries.name: Uncommitted Experiments
entries.description: Significant changes made without committing, losing rollback points.
entries.detection: Working tree >~200 changed lines or >~8 files with no commit in the session.
entries.fix_hint: Commit in atomic working increments.

entries.name: Improvement Disease
entries.description: The model changes working systems nobody asked it to touch.
entries.detection: Files modified outside the task scope; hunks unrelated to the stated change. Reader lists unrelated changes.
entries.fix_hint: Limit edits to the requested scope.

entries.name: Theater / Simulation
entries.description: Systems presented as autonomous but require hidden human intervention.
entries.detection: Reader over claimed-autonomous entrypoints; flag input()/manual prompts in code labeled automated.
entries.fix_hint: Make manual steps explicit gates or close the loop.

entries.name: Subagent Write Access
entries.description: Subagents granted direct production write rights, producing unreviewed changes.
entries.detection: Agent/tool configs granting write/exec to production paths without a review gate.
entries.fix_hint: Route subagent output through diffs/PRs with review.

entries.name: Schema Distrust
entries.description: Defensive checks re-validate data an upstream layer already guaranteed.
entries.detection: Re-validation immediately after a typed validator; repeated identical guards across call depth.
entries.fix_hint: Trust the validated boundary; assert once at the edge.

entries.name: Loose Schema Tolerance
entries.description: Non-compliant data passes through instead of being rejected.
entries.detection: Validator configs set to ignore/strip/coerce silently; parse error branch empty.
entries.fix_hint: Enforce strict validation at ingestion; reject and log.

entries.name: Multiple Truth Sources
entries.description: The same definition/constant declared in several files.
entries.detection: Same struct/enum/constant in >=2 modules; duplicate field-lists with >0.8 overlap.
entries.fix_hint: Define once in a shared module and import.

entries.name: Format Proliferation
entries.description: Multiple formats for the same data supported in parallel.
entries.detection: >=2 parser/serializer impls for the same entity, or if format==... ladders with >=3 arms.
entries.fix_hint: Pick one canonical format; convert at the boundary.

entries.name: Migration Avoidance
entries.description: Old formats left in place rather than migrated.
entries.detection: Both *_legacy and current impls for the same feature; deprecated call sites persisting N commits after a marker.
entries.fix_hint: Complete the migration and delete the legacy path.

entries.name: Test-Free Development
entries.description: Code written with no automated validation.
entries.detection: New non-trivial modules with zero corresponding test file or zero covering tests.
entries.fix_hint: Add tests covering new behavior including a failure path.

entries.name: Happy Path Only
entries.description: Tests exercise only success cases.
entries.detection: Suites with zero assertions expecting raised exceptions/error returns/boundary inputs; negative:positive ratio <0.1.
entries.fix_hint: Add tests for empty/null/oversized/malformed inputs.

entries.name: Integration Skipping
entries.description: Units tested in isolation but the integrated pipeline never exercised.
entries.detection: 100% unit-level suite (no test touches >=2 real modules); mocked deps > real deps.
entries.fix_hint: Add integration tests across the seam.

entries.name: Monolithic / Oversized Commit
entries.description: A single commit changes an unusually large number of files or lines.
entries.detection: files>30 OR added+deleted>800, or >95th percentile churn, excluding generated/vendored/merge.
entries.fix_hint: Break into smaller reviewable, revertable commits.

entries.name: Documentation Lag
entries.description: Code evolves ahead of its documentation.
entries.detection: Public symbols undocumented (<70%); signatures changed while adjacent docstring unchanged.
entries.fix_hint: Update docs in the same change that alters the interface.

entries.name: Captain's Log Neglect
entries.description: Architectural decision audit trail not updated.
entries.detection: Architecture-significant changes with no ADR/decision-log entry in the PR.
entries.fix_hint: Record an ADR for every architecture-significant decision.

entries.name: Preamble / Postamble
entries.description: Conversational explanation, apologies, or step-narration in source/comments.
entries.detection: Comment regex for LLM tells (here's, let me, as you can see, I apologize, sure!); step-narration mirroring the next line.
entries.fix_hint: Delete narration; keep only why-comments.

entries.name: Fake Acknowledgments / Phantom References
entries.description: Code/comments reference files or data that do not exist.
entries.detection: Resolve every path/URL/import in comments and code; flag unresolved.
entries.fix_hint: Verify references resolve; remove or correct.

entries.name: Delete-First Mentality
entries.description: Code deleted before being committed, making it unrecoverable.
entries.detection: Commit/working-tree deleting a file whose content was never committed; large net-deletion with no prior commit.
entries.fix_hint: Commit first, then delete in a separate revertible change.

entries.name: Hallucinated Imports & APIs
entries.description: Code imports packages or calls symbols that do not exist.
entries.detection: Cross-check imports against lockfile/registry and called symbols against the resolved package API via AST/stub resolution. The canonical deterministic check.
entries.fix_hint: Pin and verify dependencies; replace unresolved symbols.
entries.languages: Python, JS/TS; any with a registry

entries.name: Placeholder / TODO Leakage
entries.description: Generated code ships with placeholder markers meant for a human.
entries.detection: Regex: TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK, ...rest of code, your code here, lone pass/NotImplementedError. Match=warn; ellipsis-elided body=fail.
entries.fix_hint: Complete elided sections; treat placeholders as build failures.

entries.name: Inconsistent Style at the Seams
entries.description: AI-inserted code uses different conventions than the surrounding file.
entries.detection: Run the repo formatter/linter on the diff only; detect intra-file mixing (snake vs camel, quote style) in the new hunk.
entries.fix_hint: Run the project formatter; match existing conventions.

entries.name: Authoritative-But-Wrong Comments
entries.description: Comments confidently state claims the code does not do.
entries.detection: Mechanical: comment unchanged while its scope code changed; doc param/return mismatch. Reader: does this comment describe the code? CONSISTENT/INCONSISTENT.
entries.fix_hint: Make the comment match the code or delete it.
sources: Tan et al. iComment (2012); EMSE 2024 taxonomy

entries.name: Wildcard / Unpinned Dependency Versions
entries.description: Open-ended version specifiers make builds non-reproducible.
entries.detection: Manifests with *, +, latest, unpinned ^/~ on critical deps, or FROM image:latest.
entries.fix_hint: Pin exact versions; commit a lockfile.

entries.name: TODO/FIXME Density (SATD)
entries.description: Comments self-admitting incomplete/defective work.
entries.detection: Regex TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX|BUG|KLUDGE; per-KLOC density. Warn >5/KLOC; fail on tags older than configured age.
entries.fix_hint: Convert each into a tracked issue or resolve and remove.

entries.name: Obsolete / Completed TODO
entries.description: A task comment whose work has already been done.
entries.detection: Annotation pre-filter, then reader: has the task been completed? DONE/PENDING. Fallback: TODO referencing a removed symbol.
entries.fix_hint: Remove if complete; otherwise link to a tracking item.

entries.name: Redundant / Obvious Comment
entries.description: A comment restating what the code already says.
entries.detection: Normalize comment and annotated statement; flag Jaccard>=0.6 with no new token (// increment i above i++).
entries.fix_hint: Delete or replace with the why.

entries.name: Missing Docstring on Public Symbol
entries.description: An exported symbol lacks any documentation comment.
entries.detection: AST: each public declaration must have an attached doc-comment; doc-coverage %. Fail <90%.
entries.fix_hint: Add a docstring describing purpose, params, returns, raises.
entries.languages: Python, Java, Go, TS, C#, Rust

entries.name: Documented Parameter Does Not Exist
entries.description: The docstring documents a parameter absent from the signature.
entries.detection: AST+doc parse: documented param set minus actual signature set; flag doc\sig.
entries.fix_hint: Remove or rename the stale @param.
entries.languages: Python, Java, TS, C#, PHP, Ruby

entries.name: Undocumented / Missing Parameter or Return
entries.description: A signature param, return, or raise is not documented.
entries.detection: sig\doc difference; return-tag presence; raise/throw tags vs statically-collected raises.
entries.fix_hint: Document every param, return, and raised exception.

entries.name: Documented Parameter Type Mismatch
entries.description: Documented type differs from the code annotation.
entries.detection: Normalize doc and code types (Optional[X] vs X|None) and compare. Hybrid where doc type is prose.
entries.fix_hint: Align documented type with the annotation or drop it.

entries.name: Doc Example Does Not Parse
entries.description: A doc code example fails to parse in its declared language.
entries.detection: Extract tagged fenced blocks/doctests, parse in check-only mode; flag failures. Deterministic given a pinned parser.
entries.fix_hint: Fix the snippet or mark it non-executable.

entries.name: Doc Example Output Mismatch
entries.description: An executable doc example produces output differing from the shown output.
entries.detection: Run in a pinned runtime; compare stdout/return byte-for-byte. Deterministic when runtime/seeds pinned.
entries.fix_hint: Update expected output or fix the regression.

entries.name: Doc References Nonexistent Symbol
entries.description: Documentation references a symbol that no longer exists.
entries.detection: Parse cross-ref tokens (:func:, {@link}, backtick identifiers) and resolve against the AST symbol table.
entries.fix_hint: Update to the current name or remove the stale mention.

entries.name: Endpoint in Code Missing from OpenAPI Spec
entries.description: A route in code has no entry in the OpenAPI spec.
entries.detection: Enumerate routes from framework decorators into (method,path) set; diff code\spec.
entries.fix_hint: Add the path to the spec or generate it from code.

entries.name: Spec Endpoint Missing from Code
entries.description: The spec declares a path with no implementing handler.
entries.detection: Set difference spec\code over (method,path) keys.
entries.fix_hint: Implement the endpoint or remove the obsolete path.

entries.name: Documented Request/Response Param Not in Code
entries.description: A spec param/field is absent from or differently typed than the model.
entries.detection: Extract params/fields+types from spec vs handler/validation model; flag set-diffs and type mismatches.
entries.fix_hint: Reconcile spec params/types with the request/response models.

entries.name: README Usage Drift
entries.description: README commands/flags/env vars/paths no longer match the code.
entries.detection: Extract from README: CLI flags (vs argparse/click), env vars (vs reads), paths (existence), imports (vs manifest).
entries.fix_hint: Regenerate usage from the CLI or update the stale command.

entries.name: Dead Documentation Link (Link Rot)
entries.description: A doc link points to a target that no longer resolves.
entries.detection: Relative paths/anchors resolved offline (deterministic); external URLs via HTTP HEAD behind a flag.
entries.fix_hint: Repair or remove; point external rot to an archive.

entries.name: Stale Version/Changelog Reference
entries.description: A documented version/range disagrees with the manifest or tags.
entries.detection: Extract version strings from docs vs manifest version and latest tag; flag mismatch and missing changelog entry.
entries.fix_hint: Sync documented versions; add the missing changelog entry.

entries.name: LAP: Get-Method That Does Not Return
entries.description: A get/is/has method does not return its value.
entries.detection: Name starts with get/is/has AND return is void or no reachable return.
entries.fix_hint: Rename to reflect its action, or return the implied value.

entries.name: LAP: Set-Method Returns
entries.description: A set method has a non-void return without documenting why.
entries.detection: Name starts with set AND return!=void AND no doc explaining it.
entries.fix_hint: Return void or document the fluent-builder return.

entries.name: LAP: Predicate Name Without Boolean
entries.description: An is/has/can/should name is not boolean-typed.
entries.detection: Name matches predicate prefix AND type/return not boolean-coercible.
entries.fix_hint: Return/declare boolean or drop the predicate prefix.

entries.name: LAP: Singular/Plural vs Cardinality Mismatch
entries.description: A name's number disagrees with single vs collection type.
entries.detection: Inflect the last name segment; plural name + non-collection type (or vice versa) using a deterministic pluralization table.
entries.fix_hint: Rename to match cardinality or change the type.

entries.name: LAP: Name/Type/Comment Antonyms
entries.description: Name, type, or comment use antonymous terms (start vs stop).
entries.detection: Tokenize and apply a pinned antonym lexicon; flag antonym across name/type/comment of one entity.
entries.fix_hint: Reconcile the contradicting term.

entries.name: Comment Documents Not-Implemented Condition
entries.description: A comment promises behavior the body does not implement.
entries.detection: Mechanical for @throws/null contracts (generate boundary tests). Reader for free-prose validates/ensures claims: IMPLEMENTED/NOT.
entries.fix_hint: Implement the documented behavior or correct the comment.
sources: Arnaoudova LAPD B1/B2; Tan et al. @tComment

entries.name: Vague / Non-Local / Too-Much-Information Comment
entries.description: Uninformatively vague, distant-knowledge, or excessively verbose comment.
entries.detection: TMI when comment tokens >3x code tokens; non-local when referencing a distant symbol. Vagueness needs reader: SPECIFIC/VAGUE.
entries.fix_hint: Tighten to a specific local statement.

entries.name: Attribution / Beautification Comment
entries.description: Authorship/credit or decorative banner comments.
entries.detection: Regex author|written by|@author|copyright in inline positions; banner lines of only punctuation.
entries.fix_hint: Remove inline attribution and decorative banners.

entries.name: Irrelevant Comment
entries.description: A comment unrelated to the code it is attached to.
entries.detection: Zero token overlap pre-filter; reader confirmation: RELEVANT/IRRELEVANT.
entries.fix_hint: Move to the code it describes or delete.

entries.name: Vague Commit Subject
entries.description: The commit subject conveys no specific intent.
entries.detection: Match stoplist regex (fix|update|changes|misc|wip|stuff) OR subject token count <3 after stripping a CC prefix.
entries.fix_hint: Name the specific change and component.

entries.name: WIP / Temporary Commit on Shared History
entries.description: A scratch commit survived into merged history.
entries.detection: Subject matches wip|temp|checkpoint|fixup!|squash!|do not merge on a commit reachable from default branch.
entries.fix_hint: Squash WIP/fixup into a meaningful commit before merging.

entries.name: Missing Commit Body (No Rationale)
entries.description: A non-trivial change ships with only a subject line.
entries.detection: No body AND diff > 50 lines or > 3 files. Reader optional: does the subject explain WHY?
entries.fix_hint: Add a body explaining motivation and trade-offs.

entries.name: Subject Too Long / 50-72 Violation
entries.description: Commit subject exceeds the readable limit or body unwrapped.
entries.detection: First line >72 (warn >50); non-URL body line >72.
entries.fix_hint: Subject <=50 (hard 72); wrap body at 72.

entries.name: Non-Imperative Subject Mood
entries.description: Subject in past tense or narrative instead of imperative.
entries.detection: First word matches added|fixed|fixing|updating|removing etc. Allowlist project verbs.
entries.fix_hint: Phrase as a command (Add, not Added/Adding).

entries.name: Conventional Commits Violation
entries.description: Subject lacks a valid type(scope): prefix in a CC repo.
entries.detection: Conditional on adoption: subject must match feat|fix|docs|...(scope)?(!)?: .+.
entries.fix_hint: Prefix with a valid type/scope; use ! for breaking.

entries.name: Tangled / Mixed-Concern Commit
entries.description: A single commit bundles multiple unrelated concerns.
entries.detection: Touches >=3 distinct top-level dirs AND multiple categories. Reader lists independent concerns.
entries.fix_hint: Split into atomic commits, one concern each.

entries.name: Secret Committed to History
entries.description: A credential was committed and lives in recoverable history.
entries.detection: Token-format regex (AKIA, ghp_, BEGIN PRIVATE KEY, JWT) PLUS Shannon entropy >~4.0 with keyword proximity. Optional live-verify.
entries.fix_hint: Rotate immediately; purge from history; use a secret manager.

entries.name: Large Binary Blob Committed
entries.description: A large binary committed directly into Git rather than LFS.
entries.detection: Binary blob >5MB warn / >50MB fail not tracked by LFS; known artifact extensions over threshold.
entries.fix_hint: Track with Git LFS or artifact storage; gitignore build output.

entries.name: High Change-Coupling
entries.description: Files repeatedly committed together, signaling a hidden dependency.
entries.detection: Mine association rules; flag pair with support>=5 AND confidence>=0.6-0.8, excluding same-dir and lockfiles.
entries.fix_hint: Extract the shared concern or co-locate the coupled code.
sources: Gall et al. (1998); Zimmermann et al. (2004)

entries.name: Force-Push / History Rewrite on Shared Branch
entries.description: History rewritten and force-pushed to a shared branch.
entries.detection: Reflog/server hook: non-fast-forward ref update on a protected branch; previously-present SHAs unreachable.
entries.fix_hint: Never force-push shared branches; use --force-with-lease on your own.

entries.name: Commit Describes the Diff, Not the Intent
entries.description: The message narrates the diff without explaining the reason.
entries.detection: Reader: does the message add the WHY not obvious from the diff? Pre-filter: identifiers/numbers from the diff verbatim, no rationale keyword.
entries.fix_hint: State the problem the change solves, not the lines it touches.

entries.name: Missing Issue / Ticket Reference
entries.description: A non-trivial commit lacks a tracker reference.
entries.detection: Conditional on a linking convention: no match for #\d+|[A-Z]{2,}-\d+|closes #. Exclude merges, reverts, chores.
entries.fix_hint: Reference the issue in subject or footer; enforce with a hook.

entries.name: Leftover Debug / Scratch Code Shipped
entries.description: Experimental scaffolding or debug prints that should have been removed.
entries.detection: Added hunks with console.log, print(, System.out.println, debugger;, binding.pry; new scratch files.
entries.fix_hint: Delete debug statements and scratch files before committing.

entries.name: Tracked Generated / Dependency Artifacts
entries.description: Generated outputs or installed dependencies committed instead of gitignored.
entries.detection: Tracked paths matching node_modules/, __pycache__, dist/, build/, *.class, .DS_Store, especially if a lockfile makes them reproducible.
entries.fix_hint: Add to .gitignore; git rm --cached; commit only the lockfile.

entries.name: Merge Commit on a Linear-History Policy
entries.description: A merge commit appears in a linear-history repo.
entries.detection: Conditional on a linear signal: flag commits with >=2 parents on the protected branch.
entries.fix_hint: Use rebase or squash-merge; enable require-linear-history.
sources: GitLab Docs; GitHub Require linear history

entries.name: Author/Identity Hygiene Drift
entries.description: Commits carry placeholder/inconsistent identities or lack required signatures.
entries.detection: Author email matches localhost|example.com|root@|noreply; on a sign-required branch, unsigned commits; identity not in mailmap.
entries.fix_hint: Set correct user.name/email; normalize with .mailmap; require signing.

entries.name: Assertion Roulette
entries.description: Several assertions with no explanatory messages.
entries.detection: >=2 assert* invocations omitting the message parameter.
entries.fix_hint: Add failure messages or split into single-assertion tests.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS, C#, Ruby, Go

entries.name: Eager Test
entries.description: A single test exercises several methods/behaviors.
entries.detection: >1 distinct production method invoked in one test. Reader confirms multiple behaviors vs one builder chain.
entries.fix_hint: Split into one test per behavior.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS/TS, C#, Ruby

entries.name: Mystery Guest
entries.description: A test relies on external resources not visible in its body.
entries.detection: References File/Path/Connection/Socket/getenv/absolute paths without constructing them inline.
entries.fix_hint: Inline the fixture; in-memory fakes/temp dirs.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS/TS, C#, Ruby, Go
sources: van Deursen et al.; Meszaros; tsDetect

entries.name: Resource Optimism
entries.description: A test assumes an external resource exists in a known state.
entries.detection: Uses a File/resource without an existence/precondition guard or in-test creation.
entries.fix_hint: Create the resource in setup or assert preconditions.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS/TS, C#, Ruby, Go
sources: Meszaros; van Deursen et al.; tsDetect

entries.name: Conditional Test Logic
entries.description: A test contains control-flow driving assertions.
entries.detection: Any if/switch/for/while/ternary-guarded-assert/catch-pass inside a test, excluding parameterization and assertThrows lambdas.
entries.fix_hint: Use parameterized tests, matchers; straight-line Arrange-Act-Assert.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS/TS, C#, Ruby, Go

entries.name: Sleepy Test
entries.description: A test pauses with a fixed-duration sleep to wait for async work.
entries.detection: Fixed-delay calls in test code: Thread.sleep, time.sleep, await delay(n), Go time.Sleep.
entries.fix_hint: Replace with deterministic synchronization: polling, fake clocks.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS/TS, C#, Ruby, Go

entries.name: Flaky / Erratic Test
entries.description: A test gives different results across runs depending on time/order/state.
entries.detection: Proxies: now()/Date(), unseeded random, HashMap iteration order, shared mutable fixture. Empirical: differing results across shuffled runs.
entries.fix_hint: Seed randomness, inject a fixed clock, isolate fixtures.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS/TS, C#, Ruby, Go
sources: Meszaros; Palomba et al. Test Smells and Flaky Tests

entries.name: Data Race / Unsynchronized Shared Mutable State
entries.description: Two or more threads access shared mutable state concurrently with at least one write and no synchronization, producing nondeterministic corruption.
entries.detection: Static: shared field (static/instance reachable from multiple threads) written without lock/atomic/volatile and read/written outside synchronization; non-thread-safe collection (HashMap/ArrayList/dict) shared across thread/goroutine boundaries; Go: -race-style escape of a variable to >1 goroutine without a channel/mutex. Reader confirms cross-thread reachability where the analysis is conservative.
entries.fix_hint: Guard shared state with a lock/atomic, make it immutable, confine it to one thread, or pass ownership via channels.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, C#, C/C++, Go, Rust (unsafe), Python (C-ext/threads)
sources: Go race detector; Java Concurrency in Practice (Goetz 2006); ThreadSanitizer; SpotBugs MT_CORRECTNESS

entries.name: Check-Then-Act / Non-Atomic Compound Operation
entries.description: A check and a dependent action on shared state are performed as separate steps, so another thread can change the state in between (lost update, TOCTOU).
entries.detection: AST: read of a shared field in a condition followed by a write to the same field outside one atomic/locked region (if (map.containsKey(k)) map.put(k,...); if (instance==null) instance=new...); non-atomic ++/+= on a shared counter; lazy-init without double-checked-locking discipline.
entries.fix_hint: Use an atomic compound operation (putIfAbsent, compareAndSet), hold one lock across check and act, or use a concurrent data structure.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, C#, C/C++, Go, Python
sources: Java Concurrency in Practice (Goetz 2006); SonarQube S2886; CWE-367

entries.name: Inconsistent Lock Ordering (Deadlock Risk)
entries.description: Two or more locks are acquired in different orders on different paths, risking deadlock.
entries.detection: Build a lock-acquisition order graph from nested synchronized/lock-acquire sites; flag any cycle (locks A->B on one path and B->A on another). Also flag a blocking call (I/O, join, get) made while holding a lock.
entries.fix_hint: Impose a global lock-acquisition order; reduce lock scope; avoid blocking while holding a lock; prefer lock-free structures.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, C#, C/C++, Rust, Go
sources: Java Concurrency in Practice (Goetz 2006); Helgrind/ThreadSanitizer lock-order; CWE-833

entries.name: Busy-Wait / Spin Loop
entries.description: A loop repeatedly polls a condition without yielding or blocking, burning CPU instead of waiting on a proper synchronization primitive.
entries.detection: Loop whose body only re-reads a shared flag/condition with no blocking call (no wait/await/sleep-with-backoff/condition-variable), or a while(!ready){} tight loop; spin without a memory-fenced/volatile read.
entries.fix_hint: Block on a condition variable, future, channel, or event; if spinning is required use a bounded backoff and a proper memory barrier.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, C#, C/C++, Go, Python
sources: Java Concurrency in Practice (Goetz 2006); CERT CON; performance literature

entries.name: Fire-and-Forget / Unhandled Async
entries.description: An asynchronous task (Promise, Future, goroutine, Task) is launched but never awaited or its result/error never observed, so failures vanish silently.
entries.detection: AST: a Promise-returning call whose result is discarded (no await/.then/.catch/return) at a statement position (ESLint no-floating-promises); async function called without await; bare goroutine launching work whose error channel is never read; Task/Future created and dropped. C#: async method not awaited (CS4014).
entries.fix_hint: Await the result, attach an error handler, or explicitly mark intentional fire-and-forget with a documented sink that logs failures.
sources: ESLint no-floating-promises; C# CS4014; typescript-eslint; Go vet

entries.name: Unreleased / Leaked Resource
entries.description: A resource that requires explicit release (file, socket, DB connection, lock, stream, OS handle) is acquired on a path that can return or throw before releasing it.
entries.detection: AST/dataflow: resource-acquiring call (open/connect/acquire/new FileInputStream/lock()) with at least one reachable exit (return/throw/break) before the matching close/release, and not wrapped in try-with-resources/using/with/defer/RAII. ruff/flake8 SIM115; SpotBugs OBL/OS_OPEN_STREAM; Go missing defer Close.
entries.fix_hint: Wrap acquisition in try-with-resources / using / with / defer Close() / RAII so release runs on every path.
entries.languages: Java, C#, Python, Go, C/C++, Rust (non-RAII FFI)
sources: SpotBugs OBL_UNSATISFIED_OBLIGATION; ruff SIM115; CWE-404/772; golangci-lint

entries.name: SQL / Command Injection via String Concatenation
entries.description: Untrusted input is concatenated or interpolated directly into a SQL query, shell command, or other interpreter string instead of using parameterized APIs.
entries.detection: AST/taint: a query/exec sink (execute, query, Statement, os.system, subprocess with shell=True, eval of a built string, child_process.exec) whose argument is built by concatenation/interpolation of a non-constant that traces to a parameter/request/env. Lightweight: f-string/+ assembling SQL keywords with a variable. Bandit B608/B602; ESLint security/detect-child-process.
entries.fix_hint: Use parameterized queries / prepared statements; pass argv arrays (no shell); never interpolate untrusted input into an interpreter string.
entries.languages: all (SQL, shell, OS exec, template engines)
sources: OWASP Top 10 A03 Injection; Bandit B608/B602; CodeQL js/python sql-injection; CWE-89/78

entries.name: Unsafe Dynamic Evaluation
entries.description: Code passes runtime-constructed or externally-influenced strings to a dynamic evaluator (eval, exec, Function, pickle/yaml.load, deserialization), enabling code execution and defeating static analysis.
entries.detection: AST: calls to eval/exec/compile, new Function, setTimeout/setInterval with a string, pickle.loads/yaml.load(unsafe)/Marshal.load/ObjectInputStream.readObject on untrusted input. Bandit B301/B307; ESLint no-eval; flag any non-literal argument.
entries.fix_hint: Replace eval with explicit dispatch/parsing; use safe loaders (yaml.safe_load, JSON); never deserialize untrusted data with object-graph deserializers.
sources: Bandit B301/B307; ESLint no-eval; OWASP Insecure Deserialization; CWE-502/95

entries.name: Missing Input Validation at Trust Boundary
entries.description: External input (request params, file contents, env, CLI args) is consumed and used in sensitive operations (file paths, queries, sizes, redirects) without validation, bounds, or allowlisting at the boundary.
entries.detection: Mechanical proxies: request/argv/env value flows into a path join, file open, redirect, array index, or allocation size with no intervening validation/sanitization/allowlist call; path built from input without normalization (path traversal: ../ reachable). Reader confirms the absence of an equivalent boundary check.
entries.fix_hint: Validate, normalize, and allowlist all external input at the boundary; canonicalize and confine paths; bound sizes and indices.
sources: OWASP Input Validation Cheat Sheet; CWE-20/22/601; CodeQL path-injection

entries.name: Weak / Hardcoded Cryptography
entries.description: Code uses a broken or unsuitable cryptographic primitive (MD5/SHA-1 for security, DES/ECB, small keys), a hardcoded key/IV/salt, or an insecure random source for security-sensitive values.
entries.detection: AST/regex: calls to MD5/SHA1/DES/RC4/ECB-mode APIs in a security context; hardcoded literal keys/IVs/salts/passwords passed to crypto APIs; non-CSPRNG (Math.random, random.random, rand()) used to generate tokens/keys/passwords/nonces. Bandit B303/B324/B311; SpotBugs WEAK_*; ESLint detect-pseudoRandomBytes.
entries.fix_hint: Use vetted algorithms (SHA-256+/AES-GCM/Argon2), a CSPRNG for secrets, and load keys from a secret manager, never hardcoded.
sources: Bandit B303/B311/B324; OWASP Cryptographic Failures A02; CWE-327/328/330/798

entries.name: Exception as Control Flow
entries.description: Exceptions are thrown and caught to implement ordinary expected control flow (loop termination, branch selection, presence checks) rather than to signal genuine errors.
entries.detection: AST: a try/except wrapping a normal lookup/parse whose except branch returns a normal value with no error propagation, used in a hot/expected path; catch of a broad type immediately followed by continue/break/normal-flow; throwing to exit nested loops. Reader confirms the condition is expected, not exceptional.
entries.fix_hint: Use explicit conditionals, Optional/null-object, or sentinel-returning APIs; reserve exceptions for exceptional conditions.
sources: Effective Java (Bloch) Item 69; CWE-705; SonarQube S1166

entries.name: Log-and-Rethrow / Double Handling
entries.description: An exception is both logged and rethrown (or logged at multiple layers), producing duplicate noise and obscuring the true handling point.
entries.detection: AST: a catch block that calls a logger AND then throws/re-raises the same exception; the same exception logged in nested catches along one call path. SpotBugs/SonarQube S2139.
entries.fix_hint: Either handle (log and recover) or propagate with context, not both; log once at the boundary that decides the response.
sources: SonarQube S2139; CERT ERR; common error-handling guides

entries.name: Swallowed Error Return / Unchecked Error
entries.description: A function returns an error/status that the caller ignores (Go err discarded, C return code unchecked, Promise rejection unhandled, boolean result dropped), letting failures pass silently.
entries.detection: Go: assignment to _ for an error return, or an error-returning call in expression-statement position (errcheck). C: ignored return of a fallible call. JS: unhandled rejection. Boolean/status-returning API whose result is discarded.
entries.fix_hint: Check and handle or propagate every error/status; never assign errors to the blank identifier without justification.
sources: errcheck; go vet; CWE-252; Rust must_use lint

entries.name: Overly Broad Try Scope
entries.description: A try/catch wraps a large block so the handler cannot tell which statement failed and may mask unrelated errors.
entries.detection: try block spanning > N statements (e.g. >10) or containing multiple distinct fallible operations with a single broad catch; the catch references no specifics of which call failed.
entries.fix_hint: Narrow the try to the single fallible statement; catch the specific exception type per operation.
sources: Clean Code (Martin); SonarQube exception-scope guidance; CWE-396

entries.name: Returning Null Instead of Empty/Optional
entries.description: A method returns null to indicate absence of a collection or value, forcing callers into null-checks and inviting NullPointerExceptions.
entries.detection: AST: a method whose declared return is a collection/array/string returns a literal null on some path; a value-returning method returns null where an Optional/Maybe/empty is idiomatic. Reader distinguishes meaningful-null from absence.
entries.fix_hint: Return an empty collection, Optional/Maybe, or a Null Object; reserve null only where genuinely meaningful and documented.
entries.languages: Java, C#, Kotlin, TS, Go (nil slices ok)
sources: Effective Java (Bloch) Items 54/55; Fowler Introduce Special Case; CWE-476

entries.name: N+1 Query
entries.description: Code issues one query to fetch a list and then one additional query per element instead of batching, multiplying round-trips.
entries.detection: AST: a database/HTTP call inside a loop whose iterable came from a prior query/fetch; ORM lazy-relation access inside a loop without eager-load/join/prefetch. Heuristic: query/fetch sink within a for/foreach over a collection of records.
entries.fix_hint: Batch with a single query (IN / join / eager-load / prefetch / dataloader); fetch related data in bulk before the loop.
entries.languages: all with DB/ORM/remote calls (Rails, Django, Hibernate, Sequelize, JPA)
sources: Rails Bullet gem; Hibernate N+1 docs; ORM performance literature

entries.name: Loop-Invariant Computation / Repeated Work in Loop
entries.description: An expensive computation, allocation, or lookup whose result does not change across iterations is recomputed inside the loop instead of hoisted out.
entries.detection: AST: inside a loop, a call/expression with no dependence on the loop variable that is pure or idempotent (len()/size() of an unchanged collection in the condition, regex compile, config/property read, repeated DB/IO of the same key, object allocation). Flag invariant pure subexpressions evaluated each iteration.
entries.fix_hint: Hoist the invariant computation/allocation/compile out of the loop; cache the result.
sources: Compiler optimization literature (LICM); PMD/SonarQube performance rules

entries.name: String Concatenation in Loop
entries.description: Strings are built by repeated concatenation inside a loop, causing quadratic copying instead of using a builder/buffer/join.
entries.detection: AST: += or + assignment to a string accumulator inside a loop body (s = s + x / s += x); Java string + in a loop. PMD InefficientStringBuffering/AppendCharacterWithChar; SonarQube S1643.
entries.fix_hint: Use a StringBuilder/StringBuffer/bytes.Buffer/array-join; accumulate parts and join once.
sources: PMD InefficientStringBuffering; SonarQube S1643; Effective Java Item 63

entries.name: Inefficient Membership / Nested-Loop Lookup
entries.description: Repeated membership tests against a list, or a nested loop pairing two collections, yield O(n^2) where a set/map/index gives O(n).
entries.detection: AST: in / contains / indexOf against a list/array inside a loop over another collection; nested loops over two collections joining by a key with no hashing. Flag list-membership in a loop and double-nested loops correlating elements.
entries.fix_hint: Build a set/dict/index once, then look up in O(1); replace the nested loop with a hash join.

entries.name: Unbounded Resource Acquisition
entries.description: Code reads, allocates, or fetches without a bound (entire file/result-set into memory, unpaginated remote fetch, no LIMIT, unbounded queue/cache), risking memory blowups and slow responses on large inputs.
entries.detection: AST: readAll/read()/.read() of a whole stream into memory, ORM/HTTP fetch with no limit/pagination, SELECT without LIMIT in dynamic queries, unbounded collection growth (cache/queue with no eviction/cap). Reader confirms inputs can be large in production.
entries.fix_hint: Stream/iterate in chunks; paginate remote and DB queries; bound caches/queues with a size or eviction policy.
sources: 12-factor / scalability guides; CWE-400 Uncontrolled Resource Consumption

entries.name: Mutable Default Argument
entries.description: A function uses a mutable object (list/dict/set) as a default parameter value, which is created once and shared across all calls, accumulating state between invocations.
entries.detection: AST: a default parameter value that is a mutable literal/constructor ([], {}, set(), dict(), or a mutable object). pylint W0102; ruff B006.
entries.fix_hint: Default to None (or an immutable sentinel) and create the mutable object inside the body.
entries.languages: Python (canonical); JS object defaults with care
sources: pylint W0102 dangerous-default-value; ruff B006; Python docs

entries.name: Floating-Point Equality Comparison
entries.description: Two floating-point values are compared with == / != , which is unreliable due to representation and rounding error.
entries.detection: AST: == or != where at least one operand is a float/double literal, variable, or float-returning expression; equality on a computed float. PMD/SonarQube S1244; pylint and clippy float-cmp lints.
entries.fix_hint: Compare with a tolerance (abs(a-b) < epsilon) or use a decimal/rational type for exact arithmetic.
entries.languages: Java, C#, C/C++, Python, JS, Go, Rust
sources: SonarQube S1244; clippy float_cmp; IEEE-754 guidance

entries.name: Self-Assignment / Suspicious Same-Operand Operation
entries.description: An operation has identical operands in a way that indicates a bug: self-assignment (x = x), a comparison always true/false (x == x, x != x), or a self-divide/subtract that is almost certainly a typo for another variable.
entries.detection: AST: assignment where LHS and RHS are the same lvalue; binary comparison/logical op with structurally identical operands (a && a, a == a, a | a); copy-paste-with-wrong-variable signature where an adjacent member differs. SpotBugs SA_*; clippy eq_op.
entries.fix_hint: Replace the duplicated operand with the intended variable, or remove the no-op.
entries.languages: Java, C#, C/C++, JS/TS, Go, Rust, Python

entries.name: Off-By-One / Boundary Error
entries.description: A loop bound, index, or slice is off by one (< vs <=, length vs length-1, inclusive vs exclusive), causing missed elements or out-of-bounds access.
entries.detection: Mechanical proxies: index expressions of the form arr[i+1] inside a loop ranging to length without an adjusted bound; <= length / < length-1 patterns; slice end equal to length+1. Hard cases need a reader to confirm intent against the boundary.
entries.fix_hint: Re-derive the bound from the boundary intent; prefer half-open ranges and iterator/foreach over manual indexing.

entries.name: Reinventing the Standard Library
entries.description: Hand-rolled code reimplements functionality already provided by the language standard library or a ubiquitous, already-imported utility (clamp, max/min, dedupe, group-by, date parsing, deep-clone), adding bugs and maintenance cost. Common in LLM output.
entries.detection: Pattern-match well-known reimplementation shapes against a registry (manual min/max loop, manual contains/indexOf, hand-written deep-copy, hand-rolled UUID/slugify/title-case, manual JSON building). Reader confirms a stdlib/available equivalent exists.
entries.fix_hint: Replace the hand-rolled version with the standard-library or established-utility function.
sources: Effective Java Item 59 (know and use the libraries); refactoring practice

entries.name: Inconsistent Naming Convention
entries.description: Identifiers in the same scope/module mix casing or naming styles (camelCase next to snake_case, get_X next to fetchY, abbreviation used inconsistently), so the codebase reads as stitched from different sources.
entries.detection: AST: collect identifiers per declared kind (function/var/const/class) and detect >1 casing convention within a file/module beyond the language norm; detect the same concept named two ways (cfg vs config, usr vs user) by normalized-stem clustering. Distinct from CS-100 which is diff-scoped.
entries.fix_hint: Adopt and enforce one naming convention per identifier kind; pick one term per concept and rename the variants.
sources: Clean Code (Martin); PEP 8; language style guides; linters (eslint naming-convention, pylint C0103)

entries.name: Boolean Flag Parameter
entries.description: A function takes a boolean (or enum-as-flag) parameter that selects between two behaviors, so call sites read as opaque true/false and the function does two things.
entries.detection: AST: a function whose signature has a boolean parameter that is branched on in the body to choose distinct behaviors; call sites passing a bare boolean literal (foo(x, true)). Flag boolean params that gate control flow rather than carry data.
entries.fix_hint: Split into two clearly-named functions (Remove Flag Argument), or pass a named enum/options object so call sites are self-describing.
sources: Fowler Remove Flag Argument (2018); Clean Code (Martin); Martin control coupling

entries.name: Inconsistent Abstraction Level in a Function
entries.description: A single function mixes high-level orchestration with low-level detail (a business step beside raw byte-twiddling or string parsing), violating the single-level-of-abstraction guideline and obscuring intent.
entries.detection: Reader-judged primarily: identify >1 distinct abstraction level in adjacent statements (named domain calls interleaved with primitive index/parsing/bit operations). Mechanical adjunct: a function calling well-named helpers AND containing dense primitive expressions in the same body.
entries.fix_hint: Extract the low-level detail into named helpers so each function reads at one abstraction level.
sources: Clean Code (Martin) Single Level of Abstraction; Fowler Extract Function

entries.name: Hardcoded Environment / Configuration Value
entries.description: Environment-specific values (hostnames, ports, file paths, feature flags, credentials-by-reference, region/account IDs, timeouts) are hardcoded in source instead of being read from configuration or environment, breaking portability across environments.
entries.detection: AST/regex: literal hosts/IPs/URLs (localhost, internal hostnames, http(s)://...), absolute filesystem paths, port numbers, cloud account/region IDs, and tunable timeouts embedded in application logic outside designated config files. Overlaps CS-068 but scoped to environment portability.
entries.fix_hint: Externalize to environment variables or a config layer; inject per-environment values; keep code environment-agnostic.

entries.name: Layering / Architectural Dependency Violation
entries.description: A module reaches across an intended architectural boundary the wrong way (UI/controller calls the database directly, a domain layer imports a web framework, a lower layer imports an upper layer), eroding the layered architecture.
entries.detection: Define layer/package rules (allowed dependency directions) and check the import/call graph against them; flag edges violating the declared direction. Without declared rules, heuristically flag presentation-layer files importing DB drivers or domain files importing web/IO frameworks. ArchUnit / import-linter / dependency-cruiser.
entries.fix_hint: Route the dependency through the intended layer/interface; invert the dependency; enforce the rule in CI with an architecture-fitness test.
sources: Clean Architecture (Martin 2017); ArchUnit; import-linter; dependency-cruiser; Suryanarayana Ch.5

entries.name: Skipped / Disabled Test Left in Suite
entries.description: Tests are committed in a permanently disabled state (@Ignore/@Disabled, it.skip, xit, t.Skip, @pytest.mark.skip without a condition, commented-out test body), so coverage is silently lost while the suite still appears green.
entries.detection: AST/regex: skip/ignore/disable annotations and helpers (@Disabled, @Ignore, it.skip/xit/describe.skip, test.skip, t.Skip, @pytest.mark.skip/@unittest.skip, [Ignore]) on committed tests, especially without a documented reason or tracking reference; commented-out test functions.
entries.fix_hint: Fix and re-enable the test, or delete it and track the gap in an issue; never let unconditional skips accumulate silently.

entries.name: Test Without Assertions
entries.description: A test executes code but never asserts anything, so it only checks that no exception is thrown and gives false confidence (passes as long as nothing crashes).
entries.detection: AST: a test method/function containing zero assertion calls (no assert*, expect(), require/assert, should, t.Error/t.Fatal, verify-mock) and no documented exception-expectation. Exclude tests whose entire purpose is assertThrows/expect-no-throw with an explicit expectation construct.
entries.fix_hint: Add explicit assertions on the observable outcome, or mark the test as an explicit does-not-throw check using the framework's construct.
entries.languages: Java, Kotlin, Python, JS/TS, C#, Ruby, Go
sources: testsmells.org; xUnit Test Patterns (Meszaros); PMD JUnitTestsShouldIncludeAssert

entries.name: Magic-Value Coupling Between Producer and Consumer
entries.description: Two separated modules agree on a raw literal (a status string, error code, route name, queue key, header) duplicated in both producer and consumer, so a change in one silently breaks the other.
entries.detection: Cross-file: identical non-trivial string/number literals used in a producer context (set/emit/write) in one module and a consumer context (compare/switch) in another, neither referencing a shared constant. Flag the same literal in a comparison in module A and an assignment in module B.
entries.fix_hint: Define the value once in a shared constant/enum imported by both sides; derive both producer and consumer from it.
sources: Rastogi 66 Anti-Patterns (multiple-truth-sources family); DRY; refactoring practice

entries.name: Overconfident / Fabricated User-Facing Message
entries.description: Generated code emits confident success, status, or error messages that do not reflect what the code actually verified (logs success before confirming it, reports a specific cause it never checked, claims completion of work that was stubbed).
entries.detection: Reader-judged: compare an emitted message string against the surrounding control flow (a success log on a path that did not confirm success; an error message asserting a specific cause the branch did not establish). Mechanical adjunct: success/completed/saved log statements not dominated by the operation they claim, or inside a catch/empty branch.
entries.fix_hint: Emit messages only after verifying the asserted condition; make the message reflect the actual checked outcome.
sources: Rastogi 66 Anti-Patterns (theater family); arXiv:2603.28592; error-handling guidance

summary:
Reviewed the 151-entry draft for completeness against the established literature (Fowler/Beck, Suryanarayana design smells, CK/Lanza metrics, the 66 Anti-Patterns, comment/doc-drift research, commit/VCS studies, and test-smell catalogs) and confirmed it is strong on classic, OO-design, metric, comment/documentation, commit/VCS, and test smells. The major gaps were whole requested categories with little or no coverage: concurrency, resource management, security-adjacent maintainability beyond secrets, performance, and several error-handling, naming, and general-correctness smells.

Added 34 entries (CS-152 through CS-185), preserving all existing ids and their detection/determinism/source fields:
- Concurrency (CS-152-156): data race / unsynchronized shared state, check-then-act / TOCTOU, inconsistent lock ordering (deadlock), busy-wait spin, fire-and-forget async.
- Resource management (CS-157): unreleased/leaked resource (file/socket/connection/lock).
- Security-adjacent maintainability (CS-158-161): SQL/command injection via concatenation, unsafe dynamic eval / insecure deserialization, missing input validation at trust boundary, weak/hardcoded crypto + insecure random.
- Error-handling (CS-162-166): exception as control flow, log-and-rethrow, swallowed/unchecked error return, overly broad try scope, returning null instead of empty/Optional.
- Performance (CS-167-171): N+1 query, loop-invariant recomputation, string concatenation in loop, quadratic membership/nested-loop lookup, unbounded resource acquisition.
- Correctness (CS-172-175): mutable default argument, floating-point equality, self-assignment / copy-paste-variable bug, off-by-one / boundary error.
- Maintainability/naming/design (CS-176-181, 184): reinventing the standard library, inconsistent naming convention, boolean flag parameter, inconsistent abstraction level, hardcoded environment/config value, layering/architectural dependency violation, magic-value coupling between producer and consumer.
- Testing (CS-182-183): skipped/disabled test left in suite, test without assertions.
- AI-generated correctness (CS-185): overconfident/fabricated user-facing message.

Final corpus total: 185 entries. Determinism breakdown: 137 mechanical, 8 bounded-reader (CS-055, CS-064, CS-082, CS-139, CS-179, CS-185, plus pre-existing), and 40 hybrid. Note the requested mechanical-vs-bounded framing: counting hybrid with its mechanical pre-filter as machine-startable, 177 entries have a mechanical detection path and 8 are purely bounded-reader; treating hybrid as reader-assisted gives 137 fully mechanical, 40 hybrid, 8 bounded-reader.
